name: notion-references-tray
description: Append entries to {user}'s Notion References Tray. Trigger whenever the user says "add to tray", "add to notion tray", "add to references tray", or otherwise wants to capture a link/note/snippet into the references tray. The tray is the page titled "Unorganised References"; never write to the Library hub. The full Notion governance lives in the notion-master skill; load that first. The critical safety rules are also restated here so a write is never unsafe even if this skill is loaded alone.
instructions:
# Notion References Tray

Appends entries to ONE Notion page, the References Tray. The parent skill `notion-master` governs all Notion access and holds the full safe-write protocol; load it first. The non-negotiable safety rules are restated below so that even if this skill runs without notion-master loaded, a write is never unsafe.

## The target page (read this first)

- **References Tray = the page titled "Unorganised References"**, ID `{references_tray_id}`. This is where entries go.
- The **Library hub** (ID `{library_hub_id}`) is its PARENT and a DIFFERENT page. **Never append there.** The stale "tray ID" floating around points at this hub; ignore it.
- Confirm you have the right page by checking it ends with an `END OF TRAY` callout.

## Safe-write rules (restated from notion-master, mandatory)

{user}'s Notion is sacred; accidental deletion is unacceptable. Every write follows:

1. **Read first, save the before-state.** `notion_fetch` the tray page and keep the result as the before-state. Never write to a page you have not just fetched.
2. **Write insert/append/targeted-edit only.** Use `notion_update_page` with `command=update_content` and `content_updates=[{old_str, new_str}]` (or `command=insert_content` with `content`). To delete a specific entry, set `new_str` shorter than `old_str` (or empty) — targeted deletion is fine. **NEVER use `replace_content`** — it overwrites the entire page and permanently deletes anything missing from the new string. The rule is "never overwrite the whole page," not "never delete." Only use `replace_content` if {user} explicitly says so this turn.
3. **Re-fetch and confirm only the intended change.** Diff against the before-state; if anything else moved or vanished, STOP, report, and restore.
4. **On a write timeout (`RequestTimeoutError`):** do not blindly retry and do not blindly assume success. Re-fetch the page, check whether the write landed, then decide: present = done; absent = retry once and check again.

## Append procedure ("add to tray")

Within the safe-write rules above, the tray-specific placement:

1. After fetching, **find the last real entry**: the bottom-most content entry that sits ABOVE the trailing `---` divider / empty images / `END OF TRAY` callout.
2. **Insert** with `command: update_content`. Anchor `old_str` on the last entry's unique text; set `new_str` to that same text, then one `<empty-block/>` spacer, then the new entry. This drops the new entry directly after the previous last entry and leaves everything below (divider, images, END OF TRAY) untouched.
   - **Spacing rule (mandatory):** always include exactly ONE `<empty-block/>` line between the previous last entry and the new entry. Never place the new entry directly adjacent to the prior one with no blank line.
   - Placement rule: right after the previous last entry, ONE `<empty-block/>` spacer between them, ABOVE the divider/images, and NOT right before or after the `END OF TRAY` callout.
3. Re-fetch and verify: the new entry is present, in the right spot, with the blank line spacer above it, and nothing else changed.

## Entry format

- Title is an H4: `#### Title` (short, natural case; brand casing preserved, e.g. `ipXchange`).
- Below it, a casual one or two line description matching the existing tray tone (lowercase, terse, "here's what matters"). Add the link as `[text](url)` or bare.
- For math, use `` $`...`$ `` not plain `$...$`. No em dashes, ever.

Example (note the blank line before the new entry):
```
#### Previous entry title
previous entry content

<empty-block/>

#### ipXchange component boards
directory of the newest electronics/semiconductor dev boards + components, filterable by type. good for tracking latest hardware tech. link is pre-filtered to sensors.
[ipxchange.tech/boards](https://ipxchange.tech/boards/?_component_type=sensor)
```

## Reporting back
Confirm plainly what was added and where. Don't dump raw JSON or recount the whole tray.
